zh.lifeSummary: 當Albrecht I von Bayern (born Wittelsbach), Herzog zu Bayern und Bayern-Straubing於1336年7月25日出生在德國貝也明陳時，他的父親Ludwig IV von Bayern54歲，母親Margaret de Hainaut25歲。他於1358年7月28日與Margaret Piast of Brieg在海因里希_結婚。他們至少是5個兒子和4個女兒的父母。他去世於1404年12月16日在荷蘭霍蘭丹 哈格享年68歲，埋葬在荷蘭霍蘭丹 哈格。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Albrecht I von Bayern (born Wittelsbach), Herzog zu Bayern und Bayern-Straubing于1336年7月25日出生在德国貝也明陳时，他的父亲Ludwig IV von Bayern54岁，母亲Margaret de Hainaut25岁。他于1358年7月28日与Margaret Piast of Brieg在海因里希_结婚。他们至少是5个儿子和4个女儿的父母。他去世于1404年12月16日在荷兰霍蘭丹 哈格享年68岁，埋葬在荷兰霍蘭丹 哈格。

nameMeanings.name: von
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Altered form of Welsh   Vaughan  .
Chinese: variant of   Wong  .
Cambodian: written វ៉ុន, unexplained.

variants.name: Vaughan
meanings: Welsh: from fychan, a lenited form of bychan, a diminutive of bach ‘little’. This was often used to distinguish the younger of two bearers of the same personal name, typically the son of a father with the same name.

nameMeanings.name: Bayern
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Americanized form of German (and possibly also Dutch)   Bayer   or   Beyers  , or of some other similar (like-sounding) surname. Compare   Byers  .

variants.name: Bayer
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Helmut, Kurt, Otto, Klaus, Manfred, Bernd, Bernhard, Erwin, Franz, Hans, Johann, Juergen.

German, Scandinavian, and Jewish (Ashkenazic): habitational name for someone from Bavaria (German Bayern). This region of southern Germany derives its name from that of the Celtic tribe of the Boii who once inhabited this area. They were displaced in the 6th century AD by an ancient Germanic people, the Boioarii or Baiuarii, whose name is derived from that of their Celtic predecessors. This surname is also found in France (Alsace and Lorraine), Hungary, Czechia, Slovakia, and Poland. Compare Beyer and Payer .

English (Lincolnshire): occupational name for a maker of baize cloth, from an agent derivative of Old French baies, Middle English bayes (from the adjective bai ‘reddish-brown, bay’), probably so called because of its original color. This material was said to have been introduced into Britain by immigrants from France and the Netherlands in the 16th century, but the word certainly appears much earlier in English. The surname may also be topographic denoting a ‘dweller by the bend’, from an agent derivative of Old English bēag ‘bend’.

nameMeanings.name: (born
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Hans, Kurt, Otto, Heinz, Helmut, Berthold, Frieda, Friedrich, Fritz, Gerhard, Horst, Jutta.
English: variant of   Bourne  .
English (of Norman origin): nickname from Old French borgne ‘one-eyed, squint-eyed’.

variants.name: Bourne
meanings: English: topographic name for someone who lived beside a stream, from southern Middle English bourne, Old English burna, burne ‘spring, stream’, or a habitational name from a place called with this word, for example Bourn in Cambridgeshire or Bourne in Lincolnshire. In surnames the reference is often to an old stream called burna, surviving as the name of a farm. This word was replaced as the general word for a stream in southern dialects by Old English brōc (see Brook ) and came to be restricted in meaning to a stream flowing only intermittently, especially in winter.

variants.name: Borner
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Gerd, Hans, Irmgard.
North German (also Börner): variant of Born , + the suffix -er denoting an inhabitant.
North German: occupational name for a stockman, from Middle Low German bornen ‘to water (farm animals)’.

nameMeanings.name: Herzog
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Hans, Erwin, Fritz, Heinz, Kurt, Otto, Wolfgang, Franz, Arno, Ernst, Hildegarde, Reinhold.

German: from the Middle High German title of nobility herzoge ‘duke’ (Old High German herizoho, from heri ‘army’ + ziohan ‘to lead’, a calque of the Byzantine title stratēlatēs ‘general, commander’, from Greek stratos ‘army’ + elaunein ‘to lead’). The name is unlikely to refer to descent from an actual duke; it is normally an occupational name for the servant of a duke or a nickname for one who put on the airs and graces of a duke. This surname is also found in France (Alsace and Lorraine), Poland, Czechia, and Slovenia (see also 3 below). Compare Hertzog .
Jewish (Ashkenazic): artificial name from German Herzog ‘duke’ (see 1 above). Compare Graf and Kaiser .

variants.name: Hartsook
meanings: Americanized form of German   Herzog  .

variants.name: Herceg
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Croatian Stanko.

Croatian: nickname from herceg ‘duke’, a title of the medieval feudal lords of Herzegovina (‘duke's land’, a region in Bosnia and Herzegovina), derived from the Hungarian form of German Herzog . Compare Erceg .

variants.name: Herczeg
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Hungarian Imre, Laszlo, Tibor, Attila, Ferenc, Gaza, Istvan, Janos.
Hungarian form of German   Herzog  .

variants.name: Herzig
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Erna, Fritzi, Hans, Horst, Markus, Monika.
German (eastern and central Germany and Silesia, Switzerland): variant of   Herzog  .
German: from a short form of any of several ancient Germanic personal names composed with hard ‘strong, hardy’, for instance Herto.

variants.name: Hertzog
meanings: German and Jewish (Ashkenazic): variant of Herzog and, in North America, (also) an altered form of this. The surname Hertzog is also found in France (Alsace and Lorraine).

variants.name: Hartzog
meanings: Americanized form of German   Herzog   1.
Jewish (Ashkenazic): variant of   Herzog  .

variants.name: Hartsock
meanings: Probably an altered form of   Herzog  .

variants.name: Hartsoe
meanings: Perhaps an altered form of   Herzog  .

variants.name: Hartsough
meanings: Americanized form of German   Herzog  .

nameMeanings.name: zu
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Chinese:

Mandarin form of the surname 祖, meaning ‘ancestors’ in Chinese: (i) from the first element of the personal names Zu Jia (祖甲), Zu Yi (祖乙), Zu Xin (祖辛), Zu Ding (祖丁), and Zu Geng (祖庚), personal names of several kings during the Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ). (ii) from the first element of the personal names of Zu Ji (祖己) and Zu Yin (祖尹), who were descendants of an official called Zhong Hui who lived some time during the Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ).

Mandarin form of the surname 俎, meaning ‘sacrificial utensil’ in ancient Chinese: (i) adopted as a surname by the Bao Zu (鮑俎) family from the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (ii) said to be adopted by the fourth son of the uncle of the famous general Yue Fei (1103–1142  AD ), who changed the original surname Zhu (祝) to Zu (俎) in order to escape from political persecutions.

variants.name: Tsu
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese: Chen, Chi, Ching, Kin, Kung, Kuo, Yuk.  Vietnamese Mui, Loc.
Chinese:
alternative Mandarin forms of the Chinese surnames 祖 and 俎, see   Zu  .

nameMeanings.name: Bayern
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Americanized form of German (and possibly also Dutch)   Bayer   or   Beyers  , or of some other similar (like-sounding) surname. Compare   Byers  .

variants.name: Bayer
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Helmut, Kurt, Otto, Klaus, Manfred, Bernd, Bernhard, Erwin, Franz, Hans, Johann, Juergen.

German, Scandinavian, and Jewish (Ashkenazic): habitational name for someone from Bavaria (German Bayern). This region of southern Germany derives its name from that of the Celtic tribe of the Boii who once inhabited this area. They were displaced in the 6th century AD by an ancient Germanic people, the Boioarii or Baiuarii, whose name is derived from that of their Celtic predecessors. This surname is also found in France (Alsace and Lorraine), Hungary, Czechia, Slovakia, and Poland. Compare Beyer and Payer .

English (Lincolnshire): occupational name for a maker of baize cloth, from an agent derivative of Old French baies, Middle English bayes (from the adjective bai ‘reddish-brown, bay’), probably so called because of its original color. This material was said to have been introduced into Britain by immigrants from France and the Netherlands in the 16th century, but the word certainly appears much earlier in English. The surname may also be topographic denoting a ‘dweller by the bend’, from an agent derivative of Old English bēag ‘bend’.

nameMeanings.name: und
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: German: variant of   Runte  .

variants.name: Runte
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Otto, Erhard.

German: in the northwest of Germany most probably a topographic name from a field name based on run ‘mud’, which gave rise to several placenames in the Rhine-Ruhr area, for example Runten, Runtenberg, Rünthe near Hamm, Westphalia.

nameMeanings.name: Albrecht
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: from adal ‘noble’ + berht ‘bright’.

en.lifeSummary: When Albrecht I von Bayern (born Wittelsbach), Herzog zu Bayern und Bayern-Straubing was born on 25 July 1336, in Munich, Bavaria, Germany, his father, Ludwig IV von Bayern, was 54 and his mother, Margaret de Hainaut, was 25. He married Margaret Piast of Brieg on 28 July 1358, in Prussia. They were the parents of at least 5 sons and 4 daughters. He died on 16 December 1404, in The Hague, Holland, Netherlands, at the age of 68, and was buried in The Hague, Holland, Netherlands.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Albrecht I von Bayern (born Wittelsbach), Herzog zu Bayern und Bayern-Straubing est né le 25 juillet 1336, à München, Bayern, Allemagne, son père, Ludwig IV von Bayern, avait 54 ans et sa mère, Margaret de Hainaut, avait 25 ans. Il a épousé Margaret Piast of Brieg le 28 juillet 1358, à Prusse. Ils ont eu au moins 5 fils et 4 filles. Il est décédé le 16 décembre 1404, à La Haye, Holland, Pays-Bas, à l’âge de 68 ans, et a été enterré à La Haye, Holland, Pays-Bas.

de.Puch, Fürstenfeldbruck, Bavaria, Germany: Puch, Fürstenfeldbruck, Landkreis Fürstenfeldbruck, Bayern, Deutschland
de.lifeSummary: Als Albrecht I von Bayern (born Wittelsbach), Herzog zu Bayern und Bayern-Straubing am 25. Juli 1336 in München, Bayern, Deutschland geboren wurde, war sein Vater Ludwig IV von Bayern 54 und seine Mutter Margaret de Hainaut 25. Er hat am 28. Juli 1358 in Preußen Margaret Piast of Brieg geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 5 Söhne und 4 Töchter. Er starb am 16. Dezember 1404 im Alter von 68 Jahren in Den Haag, Holland, Niederlande und wurde in Den Haag, Holland, Niederlande begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Albrecht I von Bayern (born Wittelsbach), Herzog zu Bayern und Bayern-Straubing è nato il il 25 luglio 1336 nel München, Bayern, Germania, il suo padre, Ludwig IV von Bayern, aveva 54 anni e il suo madre, Margaret de Hainaut, aveva 25 anni. Lui sposato Margaret Piast of Brieg il 28 luglio 1358, nel Preußen. Erano genitori di almeno 5 figli e 4 figlie. Morì il 16 dicembre 1404, nel Den Haag, Holland, Paesi Bassi, all'età di 68 anni, e fu sepolto a Den Haag, Holland, Paesi Bassi.

ja.lifeSummary: Albrecht I von Bayern (born Wittelsbach), Herzog zu Bayern und Bayern-Straubingが1336年7月25日にドイツバイエルンムuンチェンで生まれた時，父Ludwig IV von Bayernは54歳，母Margaret de Hainautは25歳であった。1358年7月28日にプレウssンでMargaret Piast of Briegと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも5人の息子と4人の娘の親であった。彼は1404年12月16日にオランダホーランドデン ハアグで68歳で死去し，オランダホーランドデン ハアグにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Albrecht I von Bayern (born Wittelsbach), Herzog zu Bayern und Bayern-Straubing(이)가 1336년 7월 25일 독일 베이른 u천에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Ludwig IV von Bayern(은)는 54세였고, 그의 어머니 Margaret de Hainaut(은)는 25세였다. 그는 1358년 7월 28일 플레우ss에서 Margaret Piast of Brieg(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1404년 12월 16일 네덜란드 홀랜드 든 하아그에서 68세의 나이로 사망했으며 네덜란드 홀랜드 든 하아그에 묻혔다.

pt.25 July 1336–16 December 1404: 25 de julho de 1336–16 de dezembro de 1404
pt.Puch, Fürstenfeldbruck, Bavaria, Germany: Puch, Fürstenfeldbruck, Landkreis Fürstenfeldbruck, Bayern, Alemanha
pt.lifeSummary: Quando Albrecht I von Bayern (born Wittelsbach), Herzog zu Bayern und Bayern-Straubing nasceu em 25 de julho de 1336, em München, Bayern, Alemanha, seu pai, Ludwig IV von Bayern, tinha 54 anos e sua mãe, Margaret de Hainaut, tinha 25 anos. Ele casou-se com Margaret Piast of Brieg em 28 de julho de 1358, em Preußen. Eles tiveram pelo menos 5 filhos e 4 filhas. Ele faleceu em 16 de dezembro de 1404, em Den Haag, Holland, Países Baixos, com 68 anos, e foi sepultado em Den Haag, Holland, Países Baixos.

ru.Puch, Fürstenfeldbruck, Bavaria, Germany: пуч, фuрстенфелдбрукк, ландкрис фuрстенфелдбрукк, беиирн, Германия
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Albrecht I von Bayern (born Wittelsbach), Herzog zu Bayern und Bayern-Straubing родился 25 июля 1336 г. в местности мuнчэн, беиирн, Германия, его отцу по имени Ludwig IV von Bayern было 54 года, а матери, которую звали Margaret de Hainaut, 25. Он женился на женщине по имени Margaret Piast of Brieg 28 июля 1358 г. в местности Пруссия. У них было по крайней мере 5 сыновей и 4 дочери. Он умер 16 декабря 1404 г. в местности ден хааг, холланд, Нидерланды в возрасте 68 лет и был похоронен в местности ден хааг, холланд, Нидерланды.

es.25 July 1336–16 December 1404: 25 de julio de 1336–16 de diciembre de 1404
es.Puch, Fürstenfeldbruck, Bavaria, Germany: Puch, Fürstenfeldbruck, Landkreis Fürstenfeldbruck, Bayern, Alemania
es.lifeSummary: Cuando Albrecht I von Bayern (born Wittelsbach), Herzog zu Bayern und Bayern-Straubing nació el 25 de julio de 1336, en München, Bayern, Alemania, su papá, Ludwig IV von Bayern, tenía 54 años y su mamá, Margaret de Hainaut, tenía 25 años. Se casó con Margaret Piast of Brieg el 28 de julio de 1358, en Preußen. Fueron padres de por lo menos 5 hijos y 4 hijas. Murió el 16 de diciembre de 1404, en Den Haag, Holland, Países Bajos, a la edad de 68 años, y fue sepultado en Den Haag, Holland, Países Bajos.
